skill.name: specdd-author
skill.description: Author and revise SpecDD .sdd specification files in projects that already use SpecDD.
skill.summary: Creates focused SpecDD specifications with bootstrap and scope awareness.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown code spans as Ruby or shell backtick execution and weak cryptography. Review found no command execution, weak cryptographic algorithm, network access, secrets access, or prompt injection in SKILL.md. The remaining risk is normal filesystem read and write behavior for authoring .sdd specification files.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Spans Misidentified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static external command findings are false positives. The referenced lines contain markdown inline code for filenames, paths, a related skill name, and SpecDD keywords; they do not execute shell, Ruby, or any other external command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The marked text is inside SKILL.md prose and markdown code formatting. No executable script, command substitution, shell invocation, or command runner appears in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The high severity weak cryptographic algorithm finding at line 3 is not supported by the file content. The line describes SpecDD .sdd files and contains no hashing, encryption, cipher, or cryptographic API.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is plain skill metadata. I found no cryptographic operation or algorithm reference in SKILL.md.

content.value_statement: Teams need specifications that match project authority and local constraints. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code create focused SpecDD .sdd files from the active bootstrap and spec chain.
actual_capabilities: Checks that a project has a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file before authoring specs.
Resolves bootstrap files and applies project, shared, and local rules in order.
Finds the effective spec chain for a target path, behavior, or change request.
Creates or revises .sdd files only where active specs grant write authority.
Reports which bootstrap files and specs governed the authoring work.
limitations: Does not adopt SpecDD into projects that lack .specdd/bootstrap.md.
Does not change implementation files unless the user explicitly asks for that work.
Cannot infer authority from naming conventions or nearby files alone.
Stops for user input when write authority or security scope is unclear.

use_cases.title: Add Local Feature Specs
use_cases.description: Create concise .sdd files that define ownership, behavior, and constraints for a new feature area.

use_cases.title: Refine Existing Governance
use_cases.description: Revise local specs so project rules are clearer before implementation or review begins.

use_cases.title: Prepare AI Coding Context
use_cases.description: Document target scope and governing rules before Claude, Codex, or Claude Code edits project code.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Spec
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a known project area.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-author to create a local .sdd spec for [target path]. Keep it short and include only clear ownership, constraints, and behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Revise an Existing Spec
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve a spec while preserving current authority.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-author to revise the governing spec for [target path]. Preserve valid existing rules and clarify ambiguous behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Define Scope Before Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-author to resolve the spec chain for [feature]. Add or update specs that define write authority and expected behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Spec Authority
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-author to inspect the active bootstrap and spec chain for [change request]. Report authority gaps before editing any .sdd files.

output_examples.input: Create a spec for the payments retry module.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the active bootstrap files, reads the nearest governing specs, creates a focused payments retry .sdd file, and reports its intended scope.

output_examples.input: Improve the current API spec before we implement rate limits.
output_examples.output: The assistant revises the relevant API spec with explicit behavior, constraints, and ownership while preserving existing project rules.

output_examples.input: Check whether this folder can own its own spec.
output_examples.output: The assistant resolves the spec chain, reports the authority source, and asks for direction if ownership is not granted.

best_practices: Start with a precise target path, behavior, or change request.
Keep new specs short and focused on local authority or behavior.
Review the reported bootstrap and spec chain before implementation work.
anti_patterns: Using the skill in a project that has not adopted SpecDD.
Asking it to infer ownership from file names or folder proximity.
Copying broad framework rules into every local project spec.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps author or revise SpecDD .sdd specification files in projects that already use SpecDD.

faq.question: Can it set up SpecDD in a new project?
faq.answer: No. It stops if .specdd/bootstrap.md is missing and points users to an adoption workflow.

faq.question: Will it edit source code?
faq.answer: No. It avoids implementation files unless the user explicitly asks for implementation work too.

faq.question: How does it decide where to write specs?
faq.answer: It reads the active bootstrap and governing specs, then edits only where write authority is granted.

faq.question: Does it require the SpecDD CLI?
faq.answer: No. It may consult CLI-assisted discovery when useful, but it relies on direct spec reading for exact rules.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
